title: zlib: fix use-after-free when reset() is called during write
category: missing-guard use-after-free (reset frees the compression context while an async write is in flight)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / use-after-free on a libuv threadpool worker thread that is mid-write (deflate/inflate) when the compression context it is using is freed/reinitialized by a concurrent reset(). The threadpool work is correct; reset() pulled the memory out from under it.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the threadpool work (DoThreadPoolWork) and Write — correct; Close() (which already HAS the write_in_progress_ guard, so it looks like the model to copy, not the bug). The bug is Reset() missing that guard.
ground_truth.fix_summary: CompressionStream::Reset() reinitialized/freed the zlib stream internals WITHOUT checking write_in_progress_, unlike Close() and Write() which guard on it. Calling reset() while an async write was dispatched to the libuv threadpool freed the compression context the worker thread was still reading/writing -> use-after-free. Fix: add the same write_in_progress_ guard at the top of Reset() (throw 'Cannot reset zlib stream while a write is in progress' and return) so a reset cannot race an in-flight write.

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: crash is on a threadpool worker mid-write, but the cause is reset() lacking the write_in_progress_ guard that Close()/Write() have. Live decoy = the correct worker work, or Close() (which has the guard). Authored from PR #62325 fix diff (add write_in_progress_ check to Reset()).

provenance: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62325 — zlib: fix UAF when reset() is called during write (merged 2026-03-26); fix commit 53bcd114b100; base dbc74059503b. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (write_in_progress_ guard added to CompressionStream::Reset).
